creator: hoek@freenet.hamilton.on.ca
text:
In Email, "David E. O'Brien" <obrien@Nuxi.cs.ucdavis.edu> wrote:

> This program compresses a kernel using gzip
> to reduce its disk storage requirements.
> It does not reduce the memory footprint once loaded into memory.
> You loose all the symbols, so usability is limited.
      |^^^|
     /     \
      lose


> .Sh HISTORY
> The kzip command appeared in FreeBSD 2.0.5.
> Obtained from Linux via 386BSD -- based on tools/build.c by Linus Torvalds, 
> and ported to 386bsd by Serge Vakulenko.

Consistency alert: 386BSD or 386bsd?


-- 
--
tIM...HOEk
Outnumbered?  Maybe.  Outspoken?  NEVER!
